scenarios.name: Blueprint Skepticism
scenarios.title: 📋 BLUEPRINT SKEPTICISM - FIRST TEST
scenarios.context:
Six months into the Blueprint, critics call it "another government plan that will gather dust." Media pressure mounts for visible results.

As the newly appointed DAPU Director, how do you respond?

choices.label: A) QUICK WINS CAMPAIGN
choices.description: Redirect resources to high-visibility quick wins. Show immediate progress.
choices.outcome_text: Quick wins generate positive media coverage, but long-term planning suffers.
choices.headline: MOE shows early Blueprint results - critics cautiously optimistic

choices.label: B) STAKEHOLDER ENGAGEMENT
choices.description: Invest political capital in building coalition of supporters.
choices.outcome_text: Broad coalition of educators and community leaders rally behind Blueprint vision.
choices.headline: Education community unites behind Blueprint vision

choices.label: C) DATA-DRIVEN TRANSPARENCY
choices.description: Publish detailed progress dashboards. Let data speak.
choices.outcome_text: Transparent reporting builds credibility. Some critics become cautious supporters.
choices.headline: MOE launches Blueprint progress dashboard

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: Unable to counter skepticism. Blueprint credibility erodes early.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: Blueprint dismissed as empty promises - public confidence drops

scenarios.name: East Coast Flood Response
scenarios.title: 🚨 FLOOD CRISIS - DAPU BRIEFING
scenarios.context:
The Minister needs your recommendation on how DAPU can support the Ministry's flood response.

MINISTRY PRIORITY: Restore schools ASAP

Your options as Program Manager:

choices.label: A) FAST-TRACK EMERGENCY REPAIRS
choices.description: Pull resources from other projects. Prioritize flood response above all else.
choices.outcome_text: DAPU's rapid coordination wins praise. Schools repaired faster than expected.
choices.headline: MOE's rapid flood response wins praise from affected communities

choices.label: B) DEFER TO NEXT YEAR
choices.description: Advise Minister to defer major repairs to 2015. Minimal coordination now.
choices.outcome_text: Budget saved but public frustration grows. 'Government moves too slowly' criticism persists.
choices.headline: Flood victims criticize slow education recovery

choices.label: C) MOBILIZE COMMUNITY PARTNERS
choices.description: Coordinate NGO/state involvement. Leverage political capital to unlock partnerships.
choices.outcome_text: Community-led response creates lasting partnerships. Grassroots hero status achieved.
choices.headline: Community rebuilds schools together - MOE coordination praised

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: Unable to coordinate effective response due to resource constraints.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: Education Ministry unprepared for crisis - critics

scenarios.name: VLE Usage Enforcement
scenarios.title: 📱 VLE ADOPTION STRATEGY
scenarios.context:
Teachers complain about forced Frog VLE logins just to hit KPIs. The platform shows low actual usage despite high login numbers.

How should DAPU coordinate the VLE rollout?

choices.label: A) STRICT KPI ENFORCEMENT
choices.description: Maintain login requirements. Use political capital to enforce compliance.
choices.outcome_text: Login numbers increase but teacher morale drops. 'Checkbox exercise' criticism grows.
choices.headline: Teachers protest VLE enforcement - 'We're not robots'

choices.label: B) FLEXIBLE ADOPTION APPROACH
choices.description: Invest in training and support. Let schools adopt at their own pace.
choices.outcome_text: Slower rollout but genuine adoption. Teachers appreciate the flexibility.
choices.headline: MOE takes measured approach to digital learning

choices.label: C) PILOT MODEL SCHOOLS
choices.description: Focus resources on showcase schools. Build success stories first.
choices.outcome_text: Model schools demonstrate VLE value. Other schools request to join program.
choices.headline: Pilot schools show promise of digital learning

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: VLE rollout stalls without clear direction.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: MOE's digital learning initiative loses momentum

scenarios.name: TIMSS Results Crisis
scenarios.title: 🚨 TIMSS 2015 RESULTS - WAVE 2 PRESSURE
scenarios.context:
TIMSS 2015 results show Malaysia's ranking has stagnated. Wave 2 begins with intense scrutiny on quality improvements.

MINISTER'S CONCERN: 'We need visible progress NOW.'

Your strategic response:

choices.label: A) INTENSIVE TEACHER UPSKILLING
choices.description: Launch emergency teacher training program. Focus on STEM pedagogy.
choices.outcome_text: Aggressive upskilling shows early promise but strains system capacity.
choices.headline: MOE launches intensive teacher development in response to TIMSS

choices.label: B) CURRICULUM REFINEMENT
choices.description: Revise curriculum to better align with international assessments.
choices.outcome_text: Curriculum changes implemented but face resistance from some educators.
choices.headline: MOE refines curriculum following international assessment feedback

choices.label: C) LONG-TERM STRATEGY
choices.description: Stay the course. Emphasize that transformation takes time.
choices.outcome_text: Measured response maintains stakeholder confidence in long-term vision.
choices.headline: MOE reaffirms commitment to Blueprint despite TIMSS results

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: Inability to respond to TIMSS results damages credibility.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: MOE criticized for lack of response to international assessment results

scenarios.name: New Minister Transition
scenarios.title: 🔄 NEW MINISTER - WAVE 2 BEGINS
scenarios.context:
Wave 2 begins with a new Education Minister. The new minister wants to put their stamp on the Blueprint.

How should DAPU manage this transition?

choices.label: A) PROACTIVE BRIEFING
choices.description: Invest political capital in comprehensive briefing. Show DAPU value.
choices.outcome_text: New minister impressed by DAPU capability. Strong working relationship formed.
choices.headline: DAPU earns confidence of new Education Minister

choices.label: B) SHOWCASE RESULTS
choices.description: Prepare detailed Wave 1 results. Let achievements speak.
choices.outcome_text: Wave 1 results provide strong foundation for Wave 2 mandate.
choices.headline: Blueprint Wave 1 results earn ministerial support

choices.label: C) WAIT AND SEE
choices.description: Minimal engagement. Continue existing work quietly.
choices.outcome_text: New minister sidelines DAPU in early decisions. Lost influence.
choices.headline: DAPU sidelined as new minister charts own course

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: Failed to build relationship with new minister.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: DAPU struggles to adapt to new ministerial priorities

scenarios.name: Teacher Contract Reform
scenarios.title: 📋 TEACHER CONTRACT NEGOTIATIONS
scenarios.context:
The Ministry wants to reform teacher contracts to improve flexibility and performance incentives. Teacher unions are resistant.

How should DAPU coordinate this sensitive reform?

choices.label: A) NEGOTIATE WITH UNION
choices.description: Use political capital to broker a compromise. Slow but consensual approach.
choices.outcome_text: Agreement reached after lengthy negotiations. Teachers feel heard.
choices.headline: MOE and teachers reach historic agreement on reforms

choices.label: B) IMPLEMENT UNILATERALLY
choices.description: Push through reforms quickly. Compensate with budget for implementation.
choices.outcome_text: Reforms implemented but at cost of teacher morale. Protests follow.
choices.headline: Teachers protest unilateral contract changes

choices.label: C) MAINTAIN STATUS QUO
choices.description: Advise Minister to postpone reforms. Avoid confrontation.
choices.outcome_text: Opportunity for reform lost. System remains inefficient.
choices.headline: MOE backs down on teacher contract reforms

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: Reform attempt collapses without resources to implement.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: Teacher contract reform initiative fails

scenarios.name: Rural School Infrastructure Crisis
scenarios.title: 🏚️ RURAL SCHOOLS IN CRISIS
scenarios.context:
Media exposes shocking conditions in rural schools: leaking roofs, broken toilets, no electricity. Public outrage is building.

Immediate DAPU response required:

choices.label: A) EMERGENCY ALLOCATION
choices.description: Redirect major budget to emergency repairs. Comprehensive fix.
choices.outcome_text: Massive repair program launched. Rural communities grateful.
choices.headline: MOE launches RM30M rural school rescue program

choices.label: B) PHASED APPROACH
choices.description: Spread repairs over 2 years. Use political capital to manage expectations.
choices.outcome_text: Gradual improvements begin. Some criticism of slow pace.
choices.headline: MOE commits to multi-year rural school upgrade

choices.label: C) PRIVATE SECTOR PARTNERSHIP
choices.description: Coordinate CSR programs. Use political capital to broker deals.
choices.outcome_text: Innovative public-private model emerges. Sustainable solution found.
choices.headline: Corporate Malaysia adopts rural schools

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: Crisis deepens as MOE unable to respond adequately.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: Rural schools abandoned - parents furious

scenarios.name: GE14 Aftermath Management
scenarios.title: 🗳️ NEW GOVERNMENT - POLICY REVIEW
scenarios.context:
Historic government change. New Minister Dr. Maz-Lee wants to review all Blueprint initiatives.

How should DAPU position itself?

choices.label: A) ALIGN WITH NEW PRIORITIES
choices.description: Use political capital to demonstrate DAPU's value to new government.
choices.outcome_text: DAPU proves adaptability. New reform initiatives unlocked.
choices.headline: DAPU earns trust of new administration

choices.label: B) MAINTAIN CONTINUITY
choices.description: Invest budget in demonstrating Blueprint's ongoing value.
choices.outcome_text: Blueprint framework preserved despite political change.
choices.headline: Education transformation continues despite transition

choices.label: C) MINIMAL DISRUPTION
choices.description: Keep low profile. Quietly continue existing work.
choices.outcome_text: DAPU maintains but loses some influence during transition.
choices.headline: Education reform stalls during government transition

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: DAPU unable to navigate political transition effectively.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: Education coordination unit struggles post-GE14

scenarios.name: Exam Paper Leak Crisis
scenarios.title: 📰 UPSR PAPERS LEAKED!
scenarios.context:
Science and English UPSR papers leaked online days before exam. Public outrage. Integrity of national exam at stake.

DAPU must coordinate response:

choices.label: A) RETAKE EXAM NATIONALLY
choices.description: Massive logistical effort. Restore public confidence.
choices.outcome_text: Swift action restores faith in exam system. Logistical triumph.
choices.headline: MOE's decisive response to leak wins public praise

choices.label: B) RETAKE AFFECTED STATES
choices.description: Targeted approach. Balance cost and fairness.
choices.outcome_text: Partial solution satisfies most stakeholders.
choices.headline: MOE takes measured approach to exam leak

choices.label: C) INVESTIGATE ONLY
choices.description: Focus on finding culprits. Use political capital for damage control.
choices.outcome_text: Investigation launched but public trust damaged.
choices.headline: Parents question exam integrity after leak

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: Exam system credibility collapses.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: MOE unable to respond to exam leak crisis

scenarios.name: PISA Disappointment
scenarios.title: 📉 PISA 2018 - EQUITY CONCERNS
scenarios.context:
PISA 2018 results reveal persistent achievement gaps between urban and rural students.

PUBLIC PRESSURE: 'The Blueprint promised equity. Where is it?'

Your response strategy:

choices.label: A) RURAL SCHOOL ACCELERATION
choices.description: Massive investment in rural schools. Close the gap aggressively.
choices.outcome_text: Bold rural investment shows commitment but strains budget significantly.
choices.headline: MOE launches major rural education initiative after PISA results

choices.label: B) TARGETED INTERVENTIONS
choices.description: Focus on specific gap areas. Evidence-based, cost-effective approach.
choices.outcome_text: Targeted programs show measurable impact in identified gap areas.
choices.headline: MOE implements evidence-based interventions for equity gaps

choices.label: C) REFRAME NARRATIVE
choices.description: Highlight progress made. Manage expectations on equity timeline.
choices.outcome_text: Communication strategy maintains public support despite challenges.
choices.headline: MOE emphasizes long-term equity progress amid PISA results

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: Failure to address equity concerns damages Blueprint credibility.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: PISA results expose unfulfilled Blueprint equity promises

scenarios.name: UPSR Abolishment Debate
scenarios.title: 📝 UPSR ABOLISHMENT PROPOSAL
scenarios.context:
Minister Dr. Maz-Lee proposes abolishing UPSR exam. Public divided: some celebrate reduced stress, others fear declining standards.

DAPU must coordinate implementation strategy:

choices.label: A) FULL ABOLISHMENT
choices.description: Bold reform. Use political capital to push through despite controversy.
choices.outcome_text: UPSR abolished. Reformists celebrate, traditionalists protest.
choices.headline: Malaysia abolishes primary school exam - historic reform

choices.label: B) FORMAT CHANGES ONLY
choices.description: Compromise approach. Reform exam format but keep assessment.
choices.outcome_text: Moderate reform satisfies most stakeholders.
choices.headline: MOE reforms UPSR format - balanced approach

choices.label: C) MAINTAIN WITH IMPROVEMENTS
choices.description: Keep UPSR but invest in improvements.
choices.outcome_text: Status quo maintained. Opportunity for bold reform lost.
choices.headline: MOE opts for incremental UPSR improvements

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: UPSR debate drags on without resolution.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: MOE indecisive on exam reform

scenarios.name: Digital Divide Exposed
scenarios.title: 💻 DIGITAL DIVIDE CRISIS
scenarios.context:
Study reveals shocking digital divide: 40% of students lack devices or internet. Urban-rural gap widening.

DAPU must coordinate response:

choices.label: A) MASSIVE DEVICE PROGRAM
choices.description: Major budget allocation for devices and connectivity.
choices.outcome_text: Ambitious program launched. Thousands of devices distributed.
choices.headline: MOE launches RM40M digital inclusion program

choices.label: B) CONNECTIVITY FOCUS
choices.description: Prioritize internet access over devices.
choices.outcome_text: Internet connectivity expanded to rural areas.
choices.headline: MOE focuses on bridging connectivity gap

choices.label: C) HYBRID APPROACH
choices.description: Balanced investment in devices AND connectivity.
choices.outcome_text: Comprehensive digital inclusion strategy implemented.
choices.headline: MOE's balanced digital strategy wins praise

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: Digital divide continues to widen.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: MOE fails to address digital inequality

scenarios.name: Sheraton Move Crisis
scenarios.title: ⚠️ POLITICAL CRISIS - GOVERNMENT COLLAPSE
scenarios.context:
Sheraton Move: Government collapses overnight. Minister Dr. Maz-Lee resigns. Political instability.

How should DAPU navigate this crisis?

choices.label: A) WORK WITH NEW MINISTER
choices.description: Use political capital to build relationship with incoming minister.
choices.outcome_text: DAPU maintains relevance through political storm.
choices.headline: Education coordination continues despite crisis

choices.label: B) KEEP LOW PROFILE
choices.description: Minimize activity. Wait for stability.
choices.outcome_text: DAPU maintains operations quietly. Less visible but internally efficient.
choices.headline: Education reform stalls amid political chaos

choices.label: C) RALLY STAKEHOLDER SUPPORT
choices.description: Invest in maintaining stakeholder relationships.
choices.outcome_text: Strong stakeholder support helps weather crisis.
choices.headline: Education community rallies amid political turmoil

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: DAPU loses credibility during crisis.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: Education coordination collapses with government

scenarios.name: COVID-19 Pandemic Response
scenarios.title: 🦠 PANDEMIC CRISIS - EDUCATION CONTINUITY
scenarios.context:
COVID-19 forces nationwide school closures. The entire education system must pivot to remote learning immediately.

CRISIS DIRECTIVE: 'No student left behind during lockdown.'

Your emergency response:

choices.label: A) FULL DIGITAL PIVOT
choices.description: Emergency rollout of online learning for all. Massive tech investment.
choices.outcome_text: Rapid digital shift reaches many but exposes severe digital divide.
choices.headline: MOE launches emergency online learning amid pandemic

choices.label: B) HYBRID APPROACH
choices.description: Mix of online, TV broadcasts, and printed materials. Reach all students.
choices.outcome_text: Multi-channel approach provides broader reach despite quality challenges.
choices.headline: MOE implements hybrid learning strategy during pandemic

choices.label: C) MINIMAL INTERVENTION
choices.description: Basic continuity measures. Prioritize health, accept learning loss.
choices.outcome_text: Conservative response minimizes costs but significant learning loss occurs.
choices.headline: MOE adopts cautious pandemic education response

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: Inability to respond to pandemic causes catastrophic learning disruption.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: Education system collapses during pandemic lockdown

scenarios.name: School Reopening Strategy
scenarios.title: 🏫 SCHOOL REOPENING DEBATE
scenarios.context:
Cases declining. Pressure to reopen schools. Parents divided: some want normalcy, others fear safety.

DAPU must coordinate reopening strategy:

choices.label: A) PHASED REOPENING WITH STRICT SOP
choices.description: Gradual reopening with comprehensive safety measures.
choices.outcome_text: Careful reopening builds confidence. Safety maintained.
choices.headline: MOE's cautious reopening strategy succeeds

choices.label: B) CONTINUE REMOTE LEARNING
choices.description: Keep schools closed. Invest more in online learning.
choices.outcome_text: Safety prioritized but learning gaps widen.
choices.headline: Schools remain closed - digital divide concerns grow

choices.label: C) HYBRID MODEL
choices.description: Combination of in-person and online learning.
choices.outcome_text: Flexible model satisfies most stakeholders.
choices.headline: MOE's hybrid learning model wins praise

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: Reopening chaos as MOE lacks clear plan.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: School reopening confusion angers parents

scenarios.name: Learning Loss Assessment
scenarios.title: 📉 LEARNING LOSS REVEALED
scenarios.context:
Assessment reveals massive learning loss from pandemic: students 1-2 years behind.

DAPU must coordinate recovery strategy:

choices.label: A) INTENSIVE RECOVERY PROGRAM
choices.description: Major investment in catch-up programs and remedial support.
choices.outcome_text: Comprehensive recovery program launched. Progress visible.
choices.headline: MOE's RM35M recovery program tackles learning loss

choices.label: B) EXTENDED SCHOOL YEAR
choices.description: Use political capital to extend school calendar.
choices.outcome_text: Extra school time helps but parents complain about holidays.
choices.headline: School year extended to address learning loss

choices.label: C) REMEDIAL FOCUS
choices.description: Targeted support for struggling students.
choices.outcome_text: Focused approach helps close achievement gaps.
choices.headline: MOE's targeted recovery strategy shows promise

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: Learning loss becomes permanent for many students.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: MOE fails to address pandemic learning loss

scenarios.name: UPSR Abolishment Implementation
scenarios.title: ✅ UPSR ABOLISHMENT - FINAL DECISION
scenarios.context:
Time to finalize UPSR abolishment. Alternative assessment needed.

DAPU must coordinate implementation:

choices.label: A) IMMEDIATE ABOLISHMENT
choices.description: Abolish immediately. Use political capital to manage backlash.
choices.outcome_text: Bold move divides public. Reformists celebrate.
choices.headline: UPSR abolished - parents divided on decision

choices.label: B) ONE-YEAR TRANSITION
choices.description: Gradual transition with preparation time.
choices.outcome_text: Smooth transition builds confidence in reform.
choices.headline: MOE's measured UPSR transition praised

choices.label: C) ASSESSMENT ALTERNATIVES READY
choices.description: Invest in robust alternative assessment system first.
choices.outcome_text: Well-prepared transition wins broad support.
choices.headline: MOE's alternative assessment system ready

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: UPSR abolishment stalls without clear plan.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: UPSR reform confusion angers stakeholders

scenarios.name: Post-Pandemic Recovery
scenarios.title: 🔄 POST-PANDEMIC RECOVERY PLAN
scenarios.context:
Schools reopen fully. Assessment reveals significant learning loss, especially among disadvantaged students.

RECOVERY IMPERATIVE: 'We must accelerate to make up lost ground.'

Your recovery strategy:

choices.label: A) INTENSIVE CATCH-UP PROGRAM
choices.description: Extended school hours, holiday classes, targeted tutoring for all affected students.
choices.outcome_text: Aggressive recovery program shows strong results but exhausts resources.
choices.headline: MOE launches comprehensive learning recovery initiative

choices.label: B) CURRICULUM ADJUSTMENT
choices.description: Streamline curriculum. Focus on essential competencies. Realistic expectations.
choices.outcome_text: Pragmatic curriculum changes help students catch up at sustainable pace.
choices.headline: MOE adjusts curriculum to support post-pandemic recovery

choices.label: C) GRADUAL NORMALIZATION
choices.description: Return to normal operations. Let students recover naturally over time.
choices.outcome_text: Gentle return to normalcy provides stability but slow recovery.
choices.headline: MOE opts for gradual return to pre-pandemic operations

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: Failure to address learning loss jeopardizes Blueprint targets.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: Pandemic learning loss threatens Blueprint achievement

scenarios.name: Teacher Morale Crisis
scenarios.title: 😔 TEACHER BURNOUT CRISIS
scenarios.context:
Post-pandemic teacher burnout epidemic. Resignations rising. Morale at all-time low.

DAPU must coordinate response:

choices.label: A) SALARY ADJUSTMENT
choices.description: Major budget for teacher compensation increase.
choices.outcome_text: Teachers appreciate raise but budget strained.
choices.headline: MOE announces teacher salary increase

choices.label: B) NON-MONETARY RECOGNITION
choices.description: Use political capital for recognition programs.
choices.outcome_text: Recognition helps but some want tangible benefits.
choices.headline: MOE launches teacher appreciation initiatives

choices.label: C) WORKLOAD REDUCTION
choices.description: Invest in admin support to reduce teacher burden.
choices.outcome_text: Teachers grateful for reduced administrative burden.
choices.headline: MOE tackles teacher workload crisis

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: Teacher exodus accelerates.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: Teacher crisis deepens as MOE fails to act

scenarios.name: PT3 Abolishment Process
scenarios.title: 📝 PT3 ABOLISHMENT DECISION
scenarios.context:
Following UPSR abolishment, PT3 exam also under review.

DAPU must coordinate decision:

choices.label: A) FULL ABOLISHMENT
choices.description: Complete exam reform. Use political capital.
choices.outcome_text: PT3 abolished. Exam-free lower secondary achieved.
choices.headline: Malaysia abolishes PT3 - exam reform continues

choices.label: B) SCHOOL-BASED ASSESSMENT
choices.description: Replace with school-based assessment system.
choices.outcome_text: School-based assessment empowers teachers.
choices.headline: MOE introduces school-based assessment

choices.label: C) REFORMED PT3
choices.description: Keep PT3 but reform format and reduce stakes.
choices.outcome_text: Moderate reform satisfies traditionalists.
choices.headline: MOE reforms PT3 exam format

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: PT3 debate drags on without resolution.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: MOE indecisive on PT3 future

scenarios.name: Mental Health Crisis
scenarios.title: 🧠 STUDENT MENTAL HEALTH CRISIS
scenarios.context:
Post-pandemic mental health crisis: anxiety, depression, suicide rates rising among students.

DAPU must coordinate response:

choices.label: A) COUNSELOR HIRING SURGE
choices.description: Major investment in school counselors.
choices.outcome_text: Counselor-student ratio improves dramatically.
choices.headline: MOE hires 1000 new school counselors

choices.label: B) TEACHER TRAINING
choices.description: Train teachers in mental health first aid.
choices.outcome_text: Teachers equipped to support student wellbeing.
choices.headline: MOE trains teachers in mental health support

choices.label: C) EXTERNAL PARTNERSHIPS
choices.description: Partner with NGOs and mental health organizations.
choices.outcome_text: Community partnerships provide comprehensive support.
choices.headline: MOE partners with mental health organizations

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: Mental health crisis worsens without intervention.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: Student mental health crisis ignored - critics

scenarios.name: International School Exodus
scenarios.title: 🏫 INTERNATIONAL SCHOOL EXODUS
scenarios.context:
Record numbers of middle-class parents switching children to international schools. Public system losing talent and credibility.

DU must respond to this confidence crisis:

choices.label: A) PUBLIC SCHOOL EXCELLENCE PROGRAM
choices.description: Major investment in flagship public schools to compete.
choices.outcome_text: Elite public schools attract back some families, but creates two-tier system.
choices.headline: MOE creates flagship public schools to stem exodus

choices.label: B) SYSTEMIC QUALITY UPLIFT
choices.description: Spread investment across all schools. Raise baseline quality.
choices.outcome_text: Broad quality improvements begin, but results take time to show.
choices.headline: MOE invests in system-wide quality improvement

choices.label: C) REGULATE INTERNATIONAL SCHOOLS
choices.description: Use political capital to restrict international school growth.
choices.outcome_text: Regulation faces legal challenges. Parents protest restrictions.
choices.headline: MOE faces backlash over international school restrictions

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: Exodus accelerates as public system fails to respond.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: Public school confidence crisis deepens

scenarios.name: DAPU Restructured to DU
scenarios.title: 🔄 ORGANIZATIONAL RESTRUCTURE
scenarios.context:
DAPU restructured to DU (Delivery Unit). Streamlined mandate, reduced bureaucracy.

How to manage transition?

choices.label: A) EMBRACE NEW MANDATE
choices.description: Use political capital to maximize restructure benefits.
choices.outcome_text: DU emerges stronger and more efficient.
choices.headline: DU restructure improves education coordination

choices.label: B) MAINTAIN CONTINUITY
choices.description: Minimize disruption during transition.
choices.outcome_text: Smooth transition but limited improvement.
choices.headline: DU transition maintains stability

choices.label: C) MINIMAL DISRUPTION
choices.description: Business as usual approach.
choices.outcome_text: Business as usual maintains some stability but wastes restructure opportunity.
choices.headline: DU restructure fails to deliver improvements

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: Restructure creates confusion and inefficiency.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: DU restructure causes organizational chaos

scenarios.name: AI in Education Debate
scenarios.title: 🤖 AI TOOLS IN SCHOOLS
scenarios.context:
ChatGPT and AI tools revolutionizing education. Students using AI for homework. Teachers confused.

DU must coordinate policy:

choices.label: A) BAN AI TOOLS
choices.description: Prohibit AI use in schools. Traditional approach.
choices.outcome_text: Ban proves unenforceable. Students use AI anyway.
choices.headline: MOE's AI ban criticized as outdated

choices.label: B) EMBRACE & ADAPT ASSESSMENT
choices.description: Redesign assessment for AI era. Use political capital.
choices.outcome_text: Forward-thinking approach wins international praise.
choices.headline: Malaysia leads in AI-adapted education

choices.label: C) GRADUAL INTEGRATION
choices.description: Pilot AI literacy programs. Measured approach.
choices.outcome_text: Balanced approach prepares students for AI future.
choices.headline: MOE's AI integration strategy praised

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: AI debate continues without clear direction.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: MOE indecisive on AI in education

scenarios.name: Final Blueprint Review
scenarios.title: 🎯 FINAL BLUEPRINT REVIEW - 2025 APPROACH
scenarios.context:
Mid-2024 comprehensive review shows progress but gaps remain. One year left to achieve 2025 targets.

FINAL PUSH: 'This is our last chance to deliver on the Blueprint promise.'

Your final year strategy:

choices.label: A) ALL-IN ACCELERATION
choices.description: Maximum resource deployment. Hit every target possible. Legacy at stake.
choices.outcome_text: Heroic final push delivers impressive results but at significant cost.
choices.headline: MOE launches all-out effort to achieve Blueprint 2025 targets

choices.label: B) STRATEGIC FOCUS
choices.description: Prioritize achievable targets. Deliver strong finish on key metrics.
choices.outcome_text: Focused strategy delivers solid results on priority areas.
choices.headline: MOE targets key Blueprint metrics in final year push

choices.label: C) SUSTAINABLE TRANSITION
choices.description: Set up post-2025 framework. Emphasize long-term sustainability over targets.
choices.outcome_text: Forward-looking approach builds foundation for continued transformation.
choices.headline: MOE prepares sustainable post-Blueprint education framework

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: Inability to execute final year strategy undermines entire Blueprint.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: Blueprint 2025 ends with whimper as final push fails

scenarios.name: Blueprint Report Card Leak
scenarios.title: 📰 BLUEPRINT REPORT CARD LEAKED
scenarios.context:
Internal Blueprint performance report leaked to media. Public now sees raw KPI scores. Depending on your performance, this could be vindication or crisis.

How do you handle the leak?

choices.label: A) OWN THE NARRATIVE
choices.description: Hold press conference. Present full context with achievements AND gaps.
choices.outcome_text: Transparent approach wins respect. Public appreciates honesty.
choices.headline: DU earns praise for transparent Blueprint assessment

choices.label: B) ACCELERATE FINAL PUSH
choices.description: Use leak as motivation. Redirect budget to close remaining gaps.
choices.outcome_text: Urgent final push shows results but creates appearance of panic.
choices.headline: MOE scrambles to improve numbers after report card leak

choices.label: C) DAMAGE CONTROL
choices.description: Minimize attention. Question methodology. Buy time.
choices.outcome_text: Defensive response backfires. Media digs deeper.
choices.headline: MOE credibility questioned after defensive response to leak

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: No response to leak destroys public confidence.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: Blueprint credibility collapses after unanswered report card leak

scenarios.name: Blueprint Legacy Planning
scenarios.title: 📜 BLUEPRINT LEGACY
scenarios.context:
Blueprint concludes in 2025. Time to plan for post-Blueprint era.

DU must coordinate legacy planning:

choices.label: A) FORMALIZE GAINS
choices.description: Invest in institutionalizing Blueprint achievements.
choices.outcome_text: Blueprint gains locked in for future.
choices.headline: Blueprint achievements institutionalized

choices.label: B) PUBLISH COMPREHENSIVE REPORT
choices.description: Document lessons learned. Use political capital.
choices.outcome_text: Comprehensive report becomes reference for future reforms.
choices.headline: Blueprint report documents transformation journey

choices.label: C) TRAIN SUCCESSORS
choices.description: Invest in capacity building for next generation.
choices.outcome_text: Strong foundation laid for continued transformation.
choices.headline: MOE prepares next generation of education leaders

cannot_afford_penalty.outcome_text: Blueprint ends without clear succession plan.
cannot_afford_penalty.headline: Blueprint legacy uncertain as 2025 ends
